Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.
Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?
If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;
Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?
If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father is in me, and I in him.
Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
And many believed on him there.
Now a certain [man] was sick, [named] Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Martha.
(It was [that] Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
When Jesus heard [that,] he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.
Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
Then after that saith he to [his] disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.
[His] disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may awake him out of sleep.
Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall do well.
Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.
Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.
Then when Jesus came, he found that he had [lain] in the grave four days already.
Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen furlongs off:
And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat [still] in the house.
Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will give [it] thee.
Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.
Jesus said unto her, I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live:
And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die. Believest thou this?
She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son of God, which should come into the world.
And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
As soon as she heard [that,] she arose quickly, and came unto him.
Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
Jesus wept.
Then said the Jews, Behold how he loved him!
And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.
Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been [dead] four days.
Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory of God?
Then they took away the stone [from the place] where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up [his] eyes, and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me.
And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said [it,] that they may believe that thou hast sent me.
And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.
And he that was dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
Then gathered the chief priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many miracles.
If we let him thus alone, all [men] will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
And one of them, [named] Caiaphas, being the high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
And not for that nation only, but that also he should gather together in one the children of God that were scattered abroad.
Then from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death.
Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to purify themselves.
Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew [it,] that they might take him.
Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
There they made him a supper; and Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon’s [son,] which should betray him,
Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a thief, and had the bag, and bare what was put therein.
Then said Jesus, Let her alone: against the day of my burying hath she kept this.
For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed [is] the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.
These things understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and [that] they had done these things unto him.
The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
For this cause the people also met him, for that they heard that he had done this miracle.
The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the world is gone after him.
And there were certain Greeks among them that came up to worship at the feast:
The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
And Jesus answered them, saying, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone: but if it die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
He that loveth his life shall lose it; and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
If any man serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will [my] Father honour.
Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a voice from heaven, [saying,] I have both glorified [it,] and will glorify [it] again.
The people therefore, that stood by, and heard [it,] said that it thundered: others said, An angel spake to him.
Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the prince of this world be cast out.
And I, if I be lifted up from the earth, will draw all [men] unto me.
This he said, signifying what death he should die.
The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?
Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the light with you. Walk while ye have the light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
While ye have light, believe in the light, that ye may be the children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
But though he had done so many miracles before them, yet they believed not on him:
That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,
He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart; that they should not see with [their] eyes, nor understand with [their] heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
These things said Esaias, when he saw his glory, and spake of him.
Nevertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not confess [him,] lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
For they loved the praise of men more than the praise of God.
Jesus cried and said, He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
I am come a light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.
He that rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that judgeth him: the word that I have spoken, the same shall judge him in the last day.
For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I speak therefore, even as the Father said unto me, so I speak.

Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s [son,] to betray him;
Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe [them] with the towel wherewith he was girded.
Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also [my] hands and [my] head.
Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash [his] feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
If I then, [your] Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.
For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am [he. ]
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
Now there was leaning on Jesus’ bosom one of his disciples, whom Jesus loved.
Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
He then lying on Jesus’ breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a sop, when I have dipped [it.] And when he had dipped the sop, he gave [it] to Judas Iscariot, [the son] of Simon.
And after the sop Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
For some [of them] thought, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy [those things] that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
By this shall all [men] know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.
Let not your heart be troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
In my Father’s house are many mansions: if [it were] not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, [there] ye may be also.
And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?
Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.
Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the Father; and how sayest thou [then,] Shew us the Father?
Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
Believe me that I [am] in the Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater [works] than these shall he do; because I go unto my Father.
And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do [it. ]
If ye love me, keep my commandments.
And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;
[Even] the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.
I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
At that day ye shall know that I [am] in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.
He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?
Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.
He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me.
These things have I spoken unto you, being [yet] present with you.
But the Comforter, [which is] the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come [again] unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.
And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.
Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.
But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.
Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every [branch] that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.
I am the vine, ye [are] the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast [them] into the fire, and they are burned.
If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.
Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in his love.
These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and [that] your joy might be full.
This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.
Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.
Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.

Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and [that] your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.
These things I command you, that ye love one another.
If the world hate you, ye know that [it hated] me before it hated you.
If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.
Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.
He that hateth me hateth my Father also.
If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.
But [this cometh to pass,] that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.
But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, [even] the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:
And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.
These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.
They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.
But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.
Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.
And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
Of sin, because they believe not on me;
Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;
Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.
I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.

Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, [that] shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.
He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew [it] unto you.
All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew [it] unto you.
A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.

Then said [some] of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?
They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.

Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.
A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.
And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.
And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give [it] you.
Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.
At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.
I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.
His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:
As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.
And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.
I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.
Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee.
For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received [them,] and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.
I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.
And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.
And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we [are. ]
While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil.
They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.
As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.
And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.
Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;
That they all may be one; as thou, Father, [art] in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:
I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as thou hast loved me.
Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.
And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare [it:] that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.
When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.
And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.
Judas then, having received a band [of men] and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.
Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?
They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am [he.] And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
As soon then as he had said unto them, I am [he,] they went backward, and fell to the ground.
Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
Jesus answered, I have told you that I am [he:] if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:
That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.
Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.
Then said Jesus unto Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath: the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?
Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
And led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.
Now Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and [so did] another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.
But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.
Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter, Art not thou also [one] of this man’s disciples? He saith, I am not.
And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.
The high priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.
Jesus answered him, I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.
Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.
And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the high priest so?
Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.
And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. They said therefore unto him, Art not thou also [one] of his disciples? He denied [it,] and said, I am not.
One of the servants of the high priest, being [his] kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?
Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.
Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the passover.
Pilate then went out unto them, and said, What accusation bring ye against this man?
They answered and said unto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.
Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:
That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.
Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him, Art thou the King of the Jews?
Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?
Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?
Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.

Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.
Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no fault [at all. ]
But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.
Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged [him. ]
And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put [it] on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,
And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.
Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.
Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And [Pilate] saith unto them, Behold the man!
When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify [him,] crucify [him.] Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify [him:] for I find no fault in him.
The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.
When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;
And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.
Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?
Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power [at all] against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.
And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar’s friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar.
When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.
And it was the preparation of the passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King!
But they cried out, Away with [him,] away with [him,] crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Caesar.
Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led [him] away.
And he bearing his cross went forth into a place called [the place] of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha:
Where they crucified him, and two others with him, on either side one, and Jesus in the midst.
And Pilate wrote a title, and put [it] on the cross. And the writing was, JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.
This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, [and] Greek, [and] Latin.
Then said the chief priests of the Jews to Pilate, Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.
Pilate answered, What I have written I have written.
Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also [his] coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did.
Now there stood by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the [wife] of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, Woman, behold thy son!
Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own [home. ]
After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.
Now there was set a vessel full of vinegar: and they filled a spunge with vinegar, and put [it] upon hyssop, and put [it] to his mouth.
When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.

The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (for that sabbath day was an high day,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and [that] they might be taken away.
Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.
But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs:
But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.
And he that saw [it] bare record, and his record is true: and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye might believe.
For these things were done, that the scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of him shall not be broken.
And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they pierced.

And after this Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave [him] leave. He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.
And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound [weight. ]
Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.
Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews’ preparation [day;] for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.
The first [day] of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre.

Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
And he stooping down, [and looking in,] saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
And the napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must rise again from the dead.
Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, [and looked] into the sepulchre,
And seeth two angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.

Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, Master.
Jesus saith unto her, Touch me not; for I am not yet ascended to my Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and [to] my God, and your God.
Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and [that] he had spoken these things unto her.

Then the same day at evening, being the first [day] of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and stood in the midst, and saith unto them, Peace [be] unto you.
And when he had so said, he shewed unto them [his] hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they saw the Lord.
Then said Jesus to them again, Peace [be] unto you: as [my] Father hath sent me, even so send I you.
And when he had said this, he breathed on [them,] and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
Whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted unto them; [and] whose soever [sins] ye retain, they are retained.
But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: [then] came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace [be] unto you.
Then saith he to Thomas, reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and thrust [it] into my side: and be not faithless, but believing.
And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.
Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast seen me, thou hast believed: blessed [are] they that have not seen, and [yet] have believed.
And many other signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book:
But these are written, that ye might believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have life through his name.
After these things Jesus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he [himself. ]
There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the [sons] of Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.
Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.
But when the morning was now come, Jesus stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.
Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.
And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.

Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt [his] fisher’s coat [unto him,] (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.
And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.
As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.
Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, and hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.
Jesus saith unto them, Come [and] dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.
This is now the third time that Jesus shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.

So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, [son] of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
He saith to him again the second time, Simon, [son] of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.

He saith unto him the third time, Simon, [son] of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, Feed my sheep.

Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry [thee] whither thou wouldest not.
This spake he, signifying by what death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, Follow me.
Then Peter, turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; which also leaned on his breast at supper, and said, Lord, which is he that betrayeth thee?
Peter seeing him saith to Jesus, Lord, and what [shall] this man [do? ]
Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what [is that] to thee? follow thou me.
Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what [is that] to thee?
This is the disciple which testifieth of these things, and wrote these things: and we know that his testimony is true.
And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written. Amen.

「第三日」可能指七日的「第三日」，从星期一晚上开始。因为神在创造的第三
日两次宣告「神看着是好的」（创一10、12），所以犹太人传统把「第三日」当作适
合举行婚礼的双福日（Double Blessings/210 D" ）。约一19至二12的事情
可能是在一周之内发生的，而变水为酒的神迹是这一周的总结。
•「迦拿」位于拿撒勒东北约十四公里，是拿但业的家乡（二十—2）。主耶稣的母
亲可能是这家人的亲属或好友，所以协助管理筵席（3、5节）。

textCards.title: 「第三日」可能指七日的「第三日」，从星期一晚上开始。

textCards.body: 太初有道，这与《创世记》开头的"起初，上帝创造天地"相呼应。约翰随即要表明这道就是耶稣（14节），但在这里，他先说明耶稣从亘古就与上帝同在。"道"（希腊原文Logos）这个字含有上帝的自我表达或言说的概念，这概念在《旧约》中有很悠久的历史渊源。上帝的道是大有功效的：上帝一发出言语，万物就造成（创1:3，9；诗33:6.107:20：赛55:10-11）；上帝也藉着他的言说，亲自与他的百姓建立关系（如：创15:1）。同时，约翰也表明这"道"的概念比希腊哲学概念的"道"（/ogos）更优越。希腊哲学概念的"道"是一个维系宇宙秩序的无位格、理性原则。道与上帝同在，表明道："与"上帝之间的亲密关系。道就是上帝，表明这道就是"起初"创造宇宙万物的那一位上帝。以下是构成三位一体教义的基本理念：独一真神不只有一个位格，这些位格永远存在，他们之间相互关联。从教父时期（如：阿里乌，约主后256-336年）直到今日（如：耶和华见证人会），一直有人认为就是上帝只是表明耶稣是一位神（agod），而不是独一的上帝（God）。他们的理由是， 一词的希腊原文Theos前面没有定冠词。然而，按照希腊文语法（特别是关于定冠词的"科威尔法则），这里的Theos要译成"一位上帝"，，因为没有定冠词的名词并不表示该名词必然带有不定冠词的含义（"一位神，而是表明该名词（"上帝"是用来表达特定主语（"道"的谓语（所谓"谓语式主格"。事实上，Theos的含义必须根据它的上下文来决定，而这里的上下文清楚表明，约翰所说的这"上帝"（"道"是指那位创造万物的独一真神。本书还，另见1:6.12.13.18。一样受造物是如此被造的，因为凡被造的，没有一样不是藉着他造的。

textCards.body: 约翰从他 …自己的地方，即受造的世界，转到他自己的人，即犹太人。尽管表明耶稣弥赛亚身份的证据（尤其是他所行的标记性神迹）很有说服力，但犹太人还是拒绝接纳他是弥赛亚。这是本书强调的重点之一（尤其见12:37-40）。

textCards.body: 道，约翰继续第1节序言的开场白。成了肉身，不是指"道"不再是上帝，而是指本是上帝的"，同时成"道"了人（参腓2:6-7）。道成肉身是最奇妙的历史事件：那位永恒、全能、无所不在和无比圣洁的圣子，取了人的样式，住在人中间。耶稣集神人二性于一身，他是上帝，也是人。住在我们中间，字面意为"支搭帐棚"（希腊原文skenoo），暗指上帝曾在会幕中与以色列人同住（参出25:8-9.33:7）。过去，上帝在会幕和圣殿中向他的百姓彰显他的同在：现在，上帝藉着道成肉身的耶稣基督（参约1.：17）住在他的百姓中间。因此，基督的降临实现了《旧约》中上帝在会幕和圣殿中与人同住的预表。之后，基督将藉著圣灵使教会（林前3:16）和信徒的身体（林前6:19）成为上帝的殿。关于恩典……真理，见约1:16-17耶稣是上帝：用希腊文Theos（"上帝"）指耶稣的经文约1:1 太初有道，道与上帝同在，道就是上帝。从来没有人看见（《和修》"见过"）上帝，只有在父怀里的独生子（《和修》"只有在约1:18父怀里独一的儿子"；原文直译：只有在父怀里那独特的上帝）将他表明出来。约20:28多马说："我的主，我的上帝！"列祖就是他们的祖宗，按肉体说，基督也是从他们出来的，他是在万有之上，永远罗9:5 可称颂的上帝。（《和修》"列祖是他们的，基督按肉体说也是从他们出来的。愿在万有之上的上帝被称颂，直到永远。"）阿们！……等候所盼望的福（《和修》"等候福乐多2:13的盼望"），并等候至大的上帝和我们救主耶稣基督的荣耀显现……论到子却说："上帝啊，你的宝座是永永来1:8 远远的，你的国权是正直的（《和修》"你国度的权杖是正直的权杖"）。……写信给那因我们的上帝（见《和》注）彼后1:1救主耶稣基督之义，与我们同得一样宝贵信心的人。注。上帝的荣光，呼应旧约时代上帝在会幕或圣殿中显信彰显他的同在和荣耀（如：出33:22；民14:10；申5:22）。独生子（《和修》"独一儿子"），耶稣是"上帝的儿不是指他是被造的或是被生出来的（见约1:3），而是指他的所有属性都与他的父完全相同，并与父上帝有着像父子般的关系。"独一"希腊原文是monogenes.意"独的儿子（参创22:2.12,16）。因此将这个词译为"独一"（only）或"独特"，要比"独生"（onlybegotten）更达意（后者因在英文《英王钦定本》和中文《和合本》中使用而为人熟知）。

约翰福音 1:14 研修本注释：道，约翰继续第1节序言的开场白

textCards.body: 从来没有人看见上帝，即从来没有人完全没有阻隔直接看见过上帝（参6:46），但在《旧日约》中，有些人确实见过上帝部分地启示他自己。不过，在基督身上，人可以更清楚地看见上帝（见14:6）。独生子，有些古抄本在这里用"独一儿子"，但最早的抄本用"独一上里所用的"独一"与1:14为同一个词，意"独特，独一。正如在第1节那样，约翰提到"上帝"有两个不同的位格。约翰在这序言的结束，强调他在第1节提及的内容，即作为道"的耶稣就是上帝，他已经向人类启示和表明了上帝。

从来没有人看见上帝，即从来没有人完全没有阻隔直接看见过上帝（参6:46），但在《旧日约》中，有些人确实见过上帝部分地启示他自己。不过，在基督身上，人可以更清楚地看见上帝（见14:6）。独生子，有些古抄本在这里用"独一儿子"，但最早的抄本用"独一上里所用的"独一"与1:14为同一个词，意"独特，独一。正如在第1节那样，约翰提到"上帝"有两个不同的位格。约翰在这序言的结束，强调他在第1节提及的内容，即作为道"的耶稣就是上帝，他已经向人类启示和表明了上帝。

textCards.body: 约輸是"那在旷野呼喊的声音"（见《和修》），这是引用先知以赛亚的话（赛40:3；参太3:3；可1:3；路3:4）。上帝的使者在旷野中传讲悔改和审判的信息，为《旧约》中的那位主上帝（耶和华自己）亲临他的百姓，预备道路。

textCards.body: 法利赛人是一群人数较少但极具影响力的犹太人。他们根据自己对旧约律法，以及对《圣经》没有记载的传统教导的理解，强调应当一丝不苟地遵守上帝的律法，希望藉此在上帝面前得称为义并蒙他悦纳。很多法利赛人都与耶稣作对（见太23:1-36，耶稣在那里指责他们假冒伪善），但他们当中也有一些人跟从耶稣（约3:1-5,7:50.19:38-40.参徒23:6：腓3:5），见太3:7注。

textCards.body: 比较36节。关于次日，见2:1注。耶稣藉着他的舍命，实现了逾越节羔羊和《旧约》其他献祭的预表（利1:1-5:19：林前5:7：弗5:2：来10:1-14）。藉着羔羊的血得拯救，预示耶稣将成为上帝的羔羊；藉着他的死，上帝的百姓得到最终的救赎，脱离死亡、罪恶和撒但的权势（西1:13-14：来2:14-15）。关于为罪献祭的经文，另见赛53:7和《旧约》其他经文（创22:8：利14:25,16:15-22）。在约翰后来所看见的异象中，耶稣在世界末日以得胜羔羊的形象出现，带来审判和完全的胜利（肩5:6-13,7:17，21:22-23.22:1-3），这是羔羊比喻的高潮。除去世人罪孽，指耶稣献上自己，藉着为人赎罪平息上帝的忿怒（耶稣为挽回祭；参罗3:25；来2:17：约壹2:2.4:10；彼前2:24注，3:18注）。

textCards.body: 弥赛亚（希伯来文）和基督（希腊文）这两个词都是"受膏抹"的意思，通常是指被上帝膏抹。在《新约》和早期犹太教中，"弥赛亚"是一个概括性词汇，汇聚了《旧约》中对那位将要来的"受者"的许多期望，特别是《旧约》所应许的那位出自大卫家的大君王和救主（如：撒下7:5-16；诗110:1-4：赛9:6-7）。这位"受膏者"将带领、教导并拯救上帝的百姓。表明耶稣是弥赛亚的七个神迹在《约翰福音》的前半部分，约翰藉着记载七个神迹来表明耶稣是弥赛亚（参20:30-31）：变水为酒 2:1-11治好大臣的儿子 4:46-54治好38年的病人 5:1-15使五千人吃饱 6:5-13在海面上行走* 6:16-21治好生来瞎眼的人 9:1-7使拉撒路复活 11:1-44*约翰并没有明确地把这件事看成是神迹。

textCards.body: 矶法是亚兰文，意为"磐石"（参太16:16-18；另参约1:38注）。在圣经时代，上帝经常更改人的名字，以表明对他们特别的呼召，比如：将亚伯兰改名为亚伯拉罕译了七个希伯来文或亚兰将雅各改名为以色列（见创17:5，32:28）。

约翰福音 1:42 研修本注释
矶法是亚兰文，意为"磐石"（参太16:16-18；另参约1:38注）。在圣经时代，上帝经常更改人的名字，以表明对他们特别的呼召，比如：将亚伯兰改名为亚伯拉罕译了七个希伯来文或亚兰将雅各改名为以色列（见创17:5，32:28）。

textCards.body: 在21:2也提及拿但业，他可能就是巴多罗买（Bar-Tholomaios，"多罗买的儿子"》。在所有三卷符类福音使徒名单中，巴多罗买都与腓力连在一起（太10:3：可3:18：路6:14）。律法 和众先知，通常指整本犹太教即《旧约》（如：太5:17,7:12）。

textCards.body: 上帝的儿子，指耶稣是《旧约》所预言的那位弥赛亚（撒下7:14：诗2:7：见约1:14注）。以色列的王，也是《旧约》对弥赛亚的一个称呼（如：番3:15）。这两个词也問时出现在太27:42-43。

textCards.body: 我实实在在地告诉你们，这是一个郑重的声明，强调耶稣宣告的权威性和重要性。这种表达方式在本书出现了25次。注意，上一节的间接宾语是单数"你"本节的间接宾语却是复数"你们"。看见天开使者上去下来，这使人想起创28章（特别见12节）记载的雅各的故事。天使在上帝与雅各之间的天梯上去下来，作者将耶稣与这天梯做比较：耶稣是人通向上帝更好的途径（创28:12；参来10:19-20）。耶稣在哪里，9里就成为启示上帝的"新伯特利"。耶稣不只是"人子"一个普通人，他反复宣称自己是那位人子（在福音书中出现超过80次），表明他是有史以来最伟大、最显要的人子。"人子"是弥赛亚的一个称谓，它可以追溯到《但以理书》中的那位既是人又是神的神秘人物，也就是"个像人子的"（但7:13-14），他将水远统治世上万国（参太26:64）。人子将被钉在十字架上"举起"（见约3:14注），带来上帝的启示（6:27），并以权柄施行末日的审判（5:27,9:39）。

textCards.body: 这是第三日，即耶稣遇到拿但业两天之后（1:43-51）。经文继续叙述耶稣整周的活动（关于"次日"见1:29，35.43）。加利利的迦拿，考古学家非常关注位于拿撒勒以北13公里的迦拿遗址发掘场，认为那里很可能就是罗马城镇迦拿的所在地。考古学家在迦拿遗址发掘出大量的罗马陶瓷碎片，表明这里曾被罗马人占领。迎拿是罗马道路上从多利买到抹大拉之间的一个重要枢纽。

textCards.body: 耶稣称他母亲妇人（见《和》注），正如他问她的提问一样，这是一种有礼貌但咯显生疏的表达。我的时候还没有到，在本书中，耶稣的"时候°是指他被钉十字架的时候。到那时，耶稣的救赎工作藉着他代赎的死而完成（见7:30，8:20，12:23，27.13:1.17:1.另见7:30注）。在耶稣传道的这个阶段，由于以色列民对那位要来的弥赛亚有误解，因此虽然耶稣确实行了许多表明他是弥赛亚的"神迹"（见2:11注），但他仍然不向以色列人公开表明自己的身份。就连这个神迹，也是在悄然中施行的。与其他福音书相比，本书不太强调耶稣的公开传道工作，而较强调他私下对特定的人传道。

textCards.body: 神迹（原文直译：记号）是超自然的奇事，证明耶稣是弥赛亚和上帝儿子的身份，引导不信的人相信耶稣。约翰明确指出，耶稣行了这个神迹以后，他的门徒就信他了（参23节）。这是耶稣所行的头一件（《和修》"第一个"神迹，表明耶稣在孩童时期或刚成年时没有行过神迹，这与《新约》以外许多《新约次经》中的"福音故事"相反。耶稣隐藏他的神性，像常人一样生活（参7:5）。本书记载的每一个神迹都凸显耶稣是弥赛亚（参12:37-40.20:30-31），以及神迹本身的异乎寻常和引人注目，比如：满满六大缸上好的葡萄酒（2:6,10）；大臣的儿子远在家中，仅靠着耶稣话语的能力就得到医治（4:47,49-50）；被疾病折磨了38年的人得痊愈（5:5）：耶稣仅用五饼二鱼就使五千人吃饱（6:13）：生来膳眼的人重见光明（9:1-2）；以及埋在坟墓里已经四天的拉撒路复活（11:17.39）。显出他的荣耀来，这个神迹彰显耶稣的荣耀，表明他是至高的创造世界的掌管者，也是丰丰富富地供应其子民需要的慈愛上帝（参1:14）。

神迹（原文直译：记号）是超自然的奇事，证明耶稣是弥赛亚和上帝儿子的身份，引导不信的人相信耶稣。约翰明确指出，耶稣行了这个神迹以后，他的门徒就信他了（参23节）。这是耶稣所行的头一件（《和修》"第一个"神迹，表明耶稣在孩童时期或刚成年时没有行过神迹，这与《新约》以外许多《新约次经》中的"福音故事"相反。耶稣隐藏他的神性，像常人一样生活（参7:5）。本书记载的每一个神迹都凸显耶稣是弥赛亚（参12:37-40.20:30-31），以及神迹本身的异乎寻常和引人注目，比如：满满六大缸上好的葡萄酒（2:6,10）；大臣的儿子远在家中，仅靠着耶稣话语的能力就得到医治（4:47,49-50）；被疾病折磨了38年的人得痊愈（5:5）：耶稣仅用五饼二鱼就使五千人吃饱（6:13）：生来膳眼的人重见光明（9:1-2）；以及埋在坟墓里已经四天的拉撒路复活（11:17.39）。显出他的荣耀来，这个神迹彰显耶稣的荣耀，表明他是至高的创造世界的掌管者，也是丰丰富富地供应其子民需要的慈愛上帝（参1:14）。
2:11

textCards.body: 耶稣从迎拿下到迦百农，因迦拿地处山区，而迦百农则位于加利利海边。迦百农在迦拿东北约26公里、从边拿步行到迦百农大概需要一天（见可1:21注）。施洗约翰下监以后，迦百农成为耶稣传道的总部（太4:12-13：路4:28-31：参太9:1）。

textCards.body: 本书在本节首次提到一个犹太节日，并在23节首次提到犹太人的逾越节（见出12:1-28）。约翰后来提到另外两次逾越节：一次在约6:4，那次耶稣在加利利过节；另一次在11:55和12:1，那次耶稣是在耶路撒冷，也是他最后一次过逾越节。除了以上提到的这些逾越节，约翰还提到耶稣在其他犹太节期的活动，包括：5:1一个没有提到名字的节日（可能是住棚节），7:2住棚节和10:22修殿节（或称光明节）。人们从加利利上耶路撒冷去，因为耶路撒冷的海拔高于加利利，且因它是都城。

textCards.body: 殿（希腊原文hieron）指圣殿周围的区域，包括外邦人院，有别于圣殿主体建筑（希腊原文naos），后者禁止非犹太人进入。售卖牛、羊和鸽子的商人，钱的人，为远道来耶路撒冷献祭的人提供方便，让他们可以当场购买祭牲，而不必从很远的地方带来。然而，在圣殿范围内做生意，却扰乱了敬畏上帝的非犹太人的崇拜（见12:20注），妨碍了圣殿发挥它的真正功用。

textCards.body: 我……要再建立起来，暗示耶稣也参与使自己从死里复活这件事，虽然其他经文提到父和圣灵也参与其中（见徒2:24：罗6:4；林前6:14：加1:1：弗1:20；另见罗1:48.11：约10:17注）。事实上，耶稣说他（11:25：见《和修》注）。

textCards.body: 四十六年，大希律从主前20年（或19年）到18年（或17年），建造圣殿的主体（希腊原文naos，见2:14注），但主体外围更大的圣殿区域（希腊原文hieron），直到主后66年才建完。有些学者支持另一种译法："这圣殿（希腊原文naos）是四十六年前建造的"'，暗示犹太人说这话时是主后29或30年，因为不存在公元0年（见《圣经文集》中的《耶稣被钉十字架的年份》）。

textCards.body: 尼哥底母夜里来见耶稣。本书其他几个地方出现的"夜里"，通常含有属灵黑暗的象征意义（9:4，11:10.13:30：但21:3除外）。众所周知，耶稣没有受过正式的拉比训练（参7:15），所以尼哥底母身为"以色列人的先生"（3:10：《和修》"以色列人的老师"，称耶稣为拉师），表明他尊敬耶稣。这里的神迹可能包括耶稣在耶路撒冷所行的许多神迹（参2:23）。

textCards.body: 升…天，大概指"进人上帝所在的地方并留在那里"。耶稣从天降下，不是指他放弃他无所不在的神性位格，完全割断与圣父的相交，而是他现在既是神又是人，他的活动就集中于地上的生活。

textCards.body: 这是本书第一次提到人子被举起来。本书三次提到人子将来要被"举起"（参8:28,12:32）.每一次都具有约翰用词的典型特点，即一语双关（见以下经文注释：4:10,8:24,11:50-51.19:19，另参3:7-8）。"举起"既指耶稣的受死，也指他复活和升天进人荣耀（参徒2:33，5:31）。关于在旷野……蛇，见民21:9，但参赛52:13。

textCards.body: 本节经文是整本《圣经》对福音的最著名总结。在希腊原文中，本节开头有"因为"一词，《和》没有译出。"因为"连接上文15节，解释人怎样可以藉着相信基督"得永生"（15节）。在当时的背景下，上帝爱世人是个令人霞惊的说法，因为《旧约》及其他犹太著作中，只讲到上帝爱他的百姓以色列人。因为上帝爱"世人"以"一切"（15节）信基督的人，而不单是犹太人，都能得永生。上帝对世人的爱不仅仅是一种情感，还产生具体的行动：上帝将他的"独一的儿子"（见《和修》：《和》"独生子"）赐给他们。约翰在其他地方把这行动解释为，上帝差他的儿子降世为人（17节），受苦和受死，，见约1:14注，那里使用了同一个希腊文的人，就是所有真正相信他的人（见11：.25注），都得到上帝那份伟大的礼物——永生。不至灭亡，指在恒审判时不至灭亡；灭亡与永生是相对的。永生是永远与上帝同在，生命充满喜乐，恩福无限。那督的人得"永生"'，并且在今生今世已经经历永生的福乐：虽然人尚未完全经历这福乐，但在很大程度上已经历了其中的丰盛。

textCards.body: 差他的儿子，约翰喜欢称耶稣是父"差"来子（另见34-36节，5:19-26,6:40,8:35-36,14:13，17:1）。犹太人有一个熟悉的概念，就是受差遭的遭者自己（《米示拿论祝福》5.5：参约13:16.20）。耶稣是奉差遣者中的最佳典范（参9:7）。在20:21-22，耶稣转而差遣他的门徒（见该处经文注释）。奉差遺（无论是耶稣，还是他的跟随者）表明：委派、命令和信息都是由差遭者发出，而不是来自受差遭的人。这节经文指基督第一次降临。他再来的时候将要审判世人（5:27-29）。

textCards.body: 那些不信和不信靠基督的人，在上帝面前没有希望，也不能对基督置身事外。在上帝面前，他们的罪已经定了，因为他们不相信上帝解决罪咎的办法，即不信上帝独生子（《和修》"上帝独一儿子"）。此外，有些人认为真诚的人信仰任何宗教都能得到与上帝同在的永恒生命，这节经文明确驳斥这种谬论（参14:6；徒4:12；罗10:13-17；提前2:5-6；关于《旧约》那些盼望基督的信徒，见约8:56；罗4:1-24；来11:13，26）。

textCards.body: 耶稣施洗，指耶稣监督他的门徒施洗（见4:2）。他们继续施行约翰的洗礼，这洗礼建基于悔改，象征罪得洗净（见1:28注）。

textCards.body: 施洗约翰称耶稣为新郎（参平行经文太9:15），表明耶稣途经撒玛利耶稣和门徒从耶路撒冷返回加利利时，选择走一条捷径，经过撒玛利亚，中途停留叙加小镇休息。叙加靠近古城示剑：示剑在新约时代已不存在了。就在这个地方，耶稣与撒玛利亚妇人在井旁谈道。耶稣是以色列人盼望已久的君王和弥赛亚。在《旧约》中，以色列民族常被描写成上帝的"新娘"（如：赛62:4-5：耶2:2；何2:16-20）。施洗约瀚的角色是新郎的朋友，尽情地与新郎一同欢喜快乐（参约1:6-9,15.19-36）。关于耶稣是新郎，见弗5:25-27：启19:7-8。

textCards.body: 他所见所闻的，指耶稣永存在天上，以及他对上帝的本性和旨意的无限知识。没有人领受他的见证，意思很可能是只有极少人（至少在当时）领受，但还是有些人领受了（见33节）。类似的概括，见1:10.11。

textCards.body: 印上印，见6:27注。3:34参1:32-33，另见启3:1，5:6。这里论到父差造子约翰说父赐给耶稣的圣灵是没有限量的。其他人在过去和将来只是某种程度上得着圣灵的能力，但耶稣却受圣灵元限量的膏抹。

textCards.body: 父已将万有交在他手里，显示在三位一体的决议中，圣父有至高权威，圣子得着管理整个受造世界的权柄，正如《新约》中许多经文所表明的（1:3.3:16.17,5:22.6:38.14:16.28；徒2:33，5:31：罗8:29，34：林前8:615:28：弗1:4-5：来1:2.3.13：彼前3:22）。但同时，圣父、圣子、圣灵都是完全的神，三位是一体的。

textCards.body: 有永生，表明永生不仅是未来的盼望，也是现在就能经历到的。这有时被称为约翰的"实现了的末世论"。"末世论"意指末世的事件和来世的生命。根据约样的生命在现今的时代已部分"实现了"；或者说，信徒现今已部分拥有这生命。上帝的震怒常在他身上，清楚表明除非相信弥赛亚耶稣，否则无论是谁，都在上帝的审判之下（参19-21节；罗1:18-25）。

textCards.body: 耶稣必须经过这条路，是出于地理上的考虑（这是最短的路线）。但这个短语也可以表示，耶稣的行程要服从上帝主权的计划。"必须"希腊原文是dei，意为"必要"，在本书的其他地方，如3:7，14,30,9:4,10:16.20:9.通常表示从上帝而来的要求。撒玛利亚人是混血种族，是犹太人和外邦人通婚的后代，为犹太人和非犹太人所蔑视（见路10:33.17:16：约8:48：另见王下17:24-31记载，主前722年，亚述王将外邦人迁移到撒玛利亚。久而久之，他们与留居那地的一些犹太人通婚），另见约4:20-21注。经过撒玛利亚，是旅人从犹太到加利利常选的路线：然而，严谨的犹太人为了避免沾染污秽，往往会绕过撒玛利亚，宁可选择更远的路程，即波过约旦河，然后从河东边绕行。在这个犹太和加利利之间的地区，许多居民是旧约时代北国以色列的后裔。撒玛利亚人和美索不达米亚的殖民者通婚，在犹太人看来，这些撒玛利亚人已经完全被非犹太文化同化。撒玛利亚人有自己版本的摩西五经，在基利心山上有自己的圣殿（见4:20），还有他们自己编写的以色列历史。同他们的重要历史典籍一样，他们摩西五经的希伯来文抄本和亚兰文《他尔根译本》至今尚存。犹太人与撒玛利亚人的关系经常很紧张。据犹太历史学家约瑟夫记载，第1世纪革老丢在位期间，犹太人与撒玛利亚人发生激烈冲突，罗马出兵平定，并把其中许多叛乱者钉死（《犹太战记》2.232-246）。

textCards.body: 一般认为，这个叙加村庄就是坐落在以巴路山坡的阿斯卡尔，距离雅各井大约1.2公里。这一带有一些罗马时期的古墓。雅各给他儿子约瑟的那块地，故事出自创48:21-22和书24:32，这两处经文记载，雅各把示剑的士地赐给他儿子约瑟。这块地是雅各从哈抹的子孙买来的（创33:18-19），约瑟后来被埋葬于此（出13:19：书24:32）。

textCards.body: 雅各井，这口井大概位于今天的纳布卢斯，在罗马时期，这地方称为弗拉维亚尼亚波利，在《旧日约》中名叫示剑。这井曾经被拱石盖住，上面建造了一座拜占庭（第4-7世纪）式的教堂。可能因井里有瓦砾堆积之故，多年来，测量并的深度得出的结果都不一样。但按11节描述，井相当深。该井位于古代交通要道交界处和传统圣地约瑟墓附近。耶稣走路困乏，凸显他完全的人性（另见11:35.19:28）。虽然按照耶稣的神性，他是无所不能的（参1:3，10），但就耶稣的人性而言，他也会疲乏困倦。午正指正午（参《和修》），此时应该很热，是休息时间，旅人会感到口渴。妇女通常会在比较凉快的早晨或傍晚去打水（创参29:7-8），但这个生活不检点的妇女却在没有人会在井旁的时候来打水。

textCards.body: 约翰指出犹太人和撒玛利亚人没有来往，是为了向巴勒斯坦地区以外的读者解释：在许多犹太人看来，撒玛利亚人一直是不洁净的。因此人如果喝了这妇人水罐里的水，他就变成在礼仪上不洁净。这个短语中的动词"来"，也有"分享使用（物品）"这个更具体的意思。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 耶稣关于活水的话，再次运用一语双关（见以下经文注释：3:14，8:24,11:50-51.19:19，另参3:7-8）。"活水"字面意为"新鲜的泉水"（创26:19：利14:6）.但约7:38-39表明这"活水"是指圣灵住在信徒里面（参耶2:13；结47:1-6：亚14:8：另参赛12:3）。

textCards.body: 妇人提到我们的祖宗雅各，表明她和她的族人仍然认为他们是雅各（以色列，创32:28）真正的后裔。妇人显然不明白耶稣是谁，不然她就会明白耶稣甚至比以色列十二支派的祖宗雅各还大（见创49:1-28）。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 我所赐的水，就是10节提到的"活水"，在7:37-39表明那是圣灵住在信徒里面。永还不渴，人灵里那神量深切渴求认识上帝的饥渴，将会奇妙地得到永还的满足。要在他里头成为泉源，直涌到永生，使人想起赛见赛44:3,55:1-3）。

textCards.body: 与尼哥底母一样（见3:4），妇人按照字面意思理解耶稣的话，因而误会了他的意思。在本书中，耶稣经常用物质世界的有形事物（出生、水、食物、他的身体、光），来教导关于看不见的属灵世界的事情（见1678页表）。

textCards.body: 没有丈夫，严格地讲，妇人说的是真话，但她草率的回答很可能是想结束这个话题。然而，耶稣满有怜悯地对妇人循循善诱，使她认识到自己的罪，并向妇人揭示他全然知悉她的生活。双方在婚礼上彼此承诺承担婚姻的责任，会众正式认可他们所缔结的婚姻，这才是真正的婚姻的开始（见2:1；另见歌3:11；玛2:14；太9:15）。婚前性关系无疑被视不道德的行为（创38:24；出22:16；申22:13-29；太15:19：约8:41；徒15:20；林前6:18，7:2.9：帖前4:3：参林后11:2的比喻）。说明何谓信主的具体比喻约翰藉着一些具体事物，生动地阐明什么是相信耶稣。喝活水 4:10-14,7:37-386:35,47-48.吃生命的粮50-51.53-58吃耶稣的肉，喝耶稣的血 6:53-56在光中行走，得着生命的光8:12.12:35-36常住在耶稣和他的道里面 6:56,8:31.15:7

textCards.body: 你们拜父也不在这山上，也不在耶路撒冷，耶稣开创了新时代，人们不必拘泥到某城一个有形的圣殿中去耶稣教导属灵真理时所使用的具体事物尽管耶稣的听众常常误解他的教训，但约输藉帮助他们理解《约翰福音》的信息。具体事物 属灵真理对上帝的真正认识，上帝的临在；道德上的纯耶路撒冷圣殿基督的肉身从肉身生 从灵生（"重生"）风 圣灵.水 信徒里面的圣灵食物 遵行上帝的旨意粮 耶稣自己，他的生与死血和肉 耶稣的死门 藉着耶稣进入永生的道路牧人 耶稣的舍命和他对子民的看顾葡萄树 耶稣与跟随他的人的关系杯 上帝对罪的忿怒气 圣灵临到门徒敬拜上帝，而可以在任何地方敬拜，因为圣灵将住在他们里面，所以上帝在各处的子民将成为上帝居住的新圣殿（参林前3:16-17；弗2:19-22）。

textCards.body: 你们，指"你们撒玛利亚人"。我们所拜的，我们知道，耶稣这样说是把自己视为犹太人。这节经文表明本书不是反犹太人的。救恩是从犹太人出来的，指讲论救恩的整本《旧约》是从犹太人出来的，弥赛亚自己也是从犹太人，而不是从撒玛利亚人或其他外邦人出来的。

textCards.body: 上帝是个灵（《和修》"上帝是灵"，意即上帝不由任何物质构成的，没有物质的形体，但他有更奇妙的存在方式，即无所不在。因此敬拜并不限于某一个地方（节）。上帝不是免感官可以感知的（参3:6，8），但他大有能力，创造了世界（参1:1-3.10.17:5）。因为"上帝是灵"，所以以色列人不可像他们周围的民族那样，按照任何受造物的形像铸造偶像（出20:4）。

textCards.body: 在物质领域里，播种和收割之间有一段时间。但在属灵领域里，耶稣的降临已经带来了末世的收割，播种和收割同时进行，以致信徒的"庄稼"现在正收聚在上帝的国度里。这里可能指那些正出城来迎请耶稣、即将相信耶稣的撒玛利亚人（参39-42节）。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 这语句使人想起摩9:13，那里"踹葡萄的"接续"撒种的"，描述新时代的丰富和兴旺。耶稣宣称他在开创弥赛亚的时代，在其中播种和收割同时进行。快乐，看到他人相信耶稣，就有无与伦比的喜乐。

textCards.body: 耶稣离了……往并到了加利利（45节）。叙加距离迦拿至少有79公里（46节），步行至少需要两三天。

textCards.body: 迦拿，见2:1注。迦百农，见可1.21注。大臣大概是一个外邦百夫长，可能是希律安提帕（参可6:14）的手下。根据约翰的记载，耶稣先把福音带给一位备受尊敬的犹太老师（约3：|-21），接着给一个被排斥的撒玛利亚妇人（4:1-42），然后给那个为罗马政府工作的大臣（4:46-54）。以上这些例子暗示，耶稣要把福音带给世上所有的人。耶稣在加利利传道加利利是巴勒斯坦北部的一个山区，耶稣生活和传道的大部分时间都在那里度过。他在山镇拿撒勒长大，拿撒勒在外邦人的行政中心塞法里斯以南约5.6公里。耶稣开始公开传道后不久，就搬到加利利海边的迦百农居住。当时，加利利海一带的捕鱼业很发达，耶稣有好几个门徒就是渔夫。

textCards.body: 若不看见神迹奇事，你们总是不信，这挑战不只是针对大臣（耶稣就对他说），也是针对加利利人（"你们"。有些人可能只是对神迹奇事着迷，而没有看到它们是指向耶稣的，因此没有相信他（参6:2，26,30）。然而，这并不表示约瀚视"神迹"本身为负面的。相反，耶稣所行的神迹是上帝用来使人相信他的主要方法之一；神迹经常引导人跟从耶稣，或者相信他是弥赛亚（2:11，23.3:2.4:53-54,6:2.14,7:31.11:47-48.12:11.18,20:31）。

textCards.body: 池子，希伯来话叫作毕士大，"毕士大"之家"，对于那些躺在那里、极度渴望能得到奇迹般医的人来说，这个词是非常贴切的，另见1:38注。池子的名字在希腊文抄本中有许多不同的写法。尽管如此，我们有很充分的理由可以确定，这个池子是由两个池子合成一个大池，位于耶路撒冷的羊门附近，与现今的圣安娜（或译：安妮）教堂毗邻。两个池子之间有一隔墙。在这遗址周围发现的圆柱遗迹，有助于确认两个池子之间的隔墻。隔墻连同围着合成的那个大水池的四面，可能有五个廊子（《和修》"五个柱廊"，即五个有遮盖走道的柱廊廊"是一排圆柱。在这遗址上建有一座第5世纪的拜占庭式大教堂。

textCards.body: 关于《和》注："有天使按时下池子搅动那水.水动之谁先下去，无论害什么病就痊愈了"，虽然一些早期抄本有这样的描述，但在最早的抄本中却没有。因此，者认为，第4节可能不是原来《约翰福音》的一部分。然而，第7节（所有抄本都有这节）表明，当时的人相信类似的说法。

textCards.body: 病（希腊原文astheneia），根据第7节，可能指"瘫痪"、"跛足"或"极度虚弱"（该希腊用语通常表达一种"残疾"的状况）。他已经病了三十八年，38年人的寿命还长。类似的医治见太9:1-8。

textCards.body: 那天是安息日，像在安息日拿自己被褥这样的行为，《旧约》并没有明确禁止，因此这并没有违反律法的规定（参出20:8-11）。这人违反的，是后来发展出来的犹太传统。该传统制定了数以百计的繁文缛节，规定在安息日哪些事不可以做，其中有一条是禁止携带东西"从一个地方到另一个地方"（《米示拿论安息日》7.2）。耶稣没有就安息日工作的性质，进行拉比式的讨论来为自己辩护；相反，他宣称自己像上帝一样正在作工（约5:17）。正如符书11:44,45］类福音所教导的，耶稣是安息日的主（参太12:38：可2:28；路6:5）。

那天是安息日，像在安息日拿自己被褥这样的行为，《旧约》并没有明确禁止，因此这并没有违反律法的规定（参出20:8-11）。这人违反的，是后来发展出来的犹太传统。该传统制定了数以百计的繁文缛节，规定在安息日哪些事不可以做，其中有一条是禁止携带东西"从一个地方到另一个地方"（《米示拿论安息日》7.2）。耶稣没有就安息日工作的性质，进行拉比式的讨论来为自己辩护；相反，他宣称自己像上帝一样正在作工（约5:17）。正如符书11:44,45］类福音所教导的，耶稣是安息日的主（参太12:38：可2:28；路6:5）。

textCards.body: 后来耶稣在殿里遇见他，这里的"殿指圣殿建筑群，而不是圣殿本身（见2:14注），离先前那患者被医治的地方不远（毕士大池位于圣殿山以北）。不要再犯罪，可能暗示这人的病痛是由罪引起的，但并非表示人的所有苦难都是犯罪的结果（见9:2注）。

textCards.body: 我父，表示耶稣与上帝的关系亲密，其他人无法与他相比（见20:17）。当耶稣说："我父作事直到如今，我，他暗示，像父一样，他是安息日的主。这是耶稣对他神性身份的宣称，这些犹太人明白他所宣称的意义（见5:18）。创2:2-3记载，上帝在创造的第七日休息（希伯来原文shabat）。犹太拉比同意，虽然上帝在安息日一直维系着世界（也就是在"工作），但没有违犯安息日。在约7:22-23，耶稣对于在安息日治病提出不同的论证；另见9:14注。

textCards.body: 将自己和上帝当作平等，耶稣声称自己是上帝的儿子，而这种关系与普通信徒是上帝的儿女的意义不同：耶稣表明他在本质上和各方面都与上帝相同，但与上帝保持着父子般的关系（见1:14注）。如果耶稣只是一个普通人（如反对他的犹太人所认为的那样），那么耶稣这样宣称就是亵渎上帝了。

textCards.body: 耶稣声称子凭着自己不能作什么，这节和17-18节确定两个主题：（1）耶稣和上帝同等，他具有完全的神性（17-18节）；（2）父和子有不同的职责和角色（19节），子所做的一切都顺服父的指示，但这并不否定父和子根本性的平等。见以下经文注释：21、22、23节.20:28。惟有看见父所作的，可能暗示耶稣具有独特的能力，他可以在日常生活的事件中看见父上帝的作为，而那是人类通常无法看到的。

textCards.body: 父将自己所作的一切事指给耶稣看，意即耶稣意识到上帝对世上万事的掌管和旨意。根据上下文，更大的事首先是指使死人复活（11:1-45），但更重要的是包括耶稣自己的受死和复活（18-20章），以及耶稣召唤全人类进入最后的复活和审判（5:27-30）。

textCards.body: 耶稣说子也照样随自己的意思使人活着（《和修》"随自己的意愿赐人生命》，再次宣称自己的神性。耶稣做只有上帝才能做的事，因为《旧约》清楚说明，唯独上帝有权柄使死人复活和赐人生命（参申32:39；撒上2:6：王下5:7）。这"生命"既是现在赐给信徒的新"生命"（约5:24.11:25-26：林后5:17），也是基督再来时身体的复活（林前15:42-57；帖前4:13-18；见但12:2）。

textCards.body: 叫人都尊敬子如同尊敬父一样，这句话实际上确立了耶稣应受敬拜的地位，也等于宣称耶稣是上帝。不尊敬子的，就是不尊敬差子来的父，有些宗教不敬拜和尊崇耶稣，认为耶稣只不过是伟大的先知，并不代表关于上帝的真理。

textCards.body: 有永生即现在就拥有永生，这是本书中最引人注目的宣称之一。一个人相信耶稣后，永生就立即以显著的方式部分地实现了。那些相信主的人，可以满怀信心地面对最后审判（参约壹5:11-13）。

textCards.body: 在这里，耶稣不是强调最后的复活，而是表明人在今世就可以经历到永生的真实，因为他说现在就是那时候了。死人，指那些灵性死亡之人，他们听见耶稣所宣讲的信息并且相信了。

textCards.title: 约翰福音 5:25 研修本注释

textCards.body: 因为，这一节解释为什么耶稣可以向死人说话和赐给他们生命。正如父从来不是被造的，他的生命也不是由别人给予的，而是他在自己有生命（《和修》"面有生命》，因此他能把生命赐给别人；同样，子在自己有生命（《和修》"自己里面有生命"，并能使死人复活得着生命。父赐给他儿子，不是指父创造子（见1.3及注释），而是指父给子权柄可以赐生命给其他人（这节解释了5:25）。这个关于"自己有生命"的声称，呼应序言中"生命在他（耶稣）里头"的宣称（1:4，另见3:15-16.11:25：见14:6注）。

textCards.body: 因为他是人子呼应但7:13，见约1:51注。耶稣既是上帝的儿子，也是拥有真正人性的"人子"，是但7:13-14预言的、永远统治世界的那一位。父已赐他权柄，对全人类施行最后的审判。

textCards.body: 耶稣称施洗约翰为点着的明灯，呼应诗132:17，那里提到上帝为他的受膏者"预备明灯"。施洗约翰是一盏"明灯〞，但不是那光（约1:7-9）；他的见证虽然重要，但影响力较小，并且是暂时的。这里用过去时态，可能暗示此时约翰已经去世，或者至少在监狱里，另见3:28-30注。关于"灯"，见太25:3-4注。

textCards.body: 研究《圣经》本身不能给予人生命。相反，《圣经》是为赐生命的那一位（耶稣）作见证（参46-47节）。因此研究《圣经》的结果应当是真正地相信耶稣，然后有顺服的行动和更新的生命，而不单单是获取圣经知识。给我作见证的就是这经，那些读《旧约》却不明白它是指向耶稣的人（像那些反对耶稣的犹太人），就是未能明白它的信息。另见《圣经文集》中的《《圣经〉概述》。

textCards.body: 我奉我父的名来，指耶稣带着父的权柄来，更深层的意思是，耶稣彰显了父的全部属性。按照《旧约》的意思，他的"名"代表关于他的所有事实。若有别……来，耶稣预言假基督增多（太24:24）是末日的一个征兆（平行经文太24:5）。第1世纪犹太历史学家约瑟夫记载，主后70年以前，不断出现一些假冒弥赛亚的人。

textCards.body: 你们如果信摩西，也必信我，这里假设真信徒有一颗接受上帝真道的心。那些相信上帝藉着摩西所写的话（《创世记》至《申命记》）的人，也会认识和渴望接受上帝藉耶稣所说的话。那些不相信摩西所写的话的人，也不会相信耶稣。因为他书上有指着我写的话（《和修》"为他写过关于我的事"，不仅包括像申18:15那样明确的预言，还包括摩西五经所述在救恩历史中所有指向基督的话（如：路24:27，44；约1:45，3:14.8:56；徒26:22.28:23；林前10:4；来11:23-26：彼前1:10-12；犹5节；和《圣经文集》中的《〈圣经〉概述》。

textCards.body: 这事以后，指过了一些时候，但时间长度不详（参5:1）。从先前的事件到现在可能已经过了差不多半年。加利利海，就是提比哩亚海（参21:1），又称基尼烈湖。约旦河南北贯穿，水从北面流入湖里，又从南面流出。现在这个湖约宽11.3公里，长21公里。考古学家在这个湖里及其周围发掘出一些文物，包括一艘第1世纪的船（见太4:21注），以及一些沿岸城邑（包括迦百农和提比哩亚）的古迹。关于耶稣使五千人吃饱的地点，见路9:10注。关于提比哩亚城，见约6:23-24注。

约翰福音 6:1 研修本注释：这事以后，指过了一些时候，但时间长度不详（参5:1）
这事以后，指过了一些时候，但时间长度不详（参5:1）。从先前的事件到现在可能已经过了差不多半年。加利利海，就是提比哩亚海（参21:1），又称基尼烈湖。约旦河南北贯穿，水从北面流入湖里，又从南面流出。现在这个湖约宽11.3公里，长21公里。…
这事以后，指过了一些时候，但时间长度不详（参5:1）。从先前的事件到现在可能已经过了差不多半年。加利利海，就是提比哩亚海（参21:1），又称基尼烈湖。约旦河南北贯穿，水从北面流入湖里，又从南面流出。现在这个湖约宽11.3公里，长21公里。考古学家在这个湖里及其周围发掘出一些文物，包括一艘第1世纪的船（见太4:21注），以及一些沿岸城邑（包括迦百农和提比哩亚）的古迹。关于耶稣使五千人吃饱的地点，见路9:10注。关于提比哩亚城，见约6:23-24注。
6:1
圣经研修本 43_约翰福音【codex-v3】

textCards.body: 那 先知，申18:15，18预言有一位像摩西的先知要来，这里的人视耶稣就是那位应许的先知（参约1:20-21注，7:40-41注：在徒3:22-23，彼得认为耶稣应验了这预言；参徒7:37）。然而，"先知"并不是对耶稣的常见称谓，只是那些不太认识他的人才这样称呼他（如：太16:14；约4:19）。其实，耶稣比先知大多了。

textCards.body: 门徒摇橹约行了十里多路，如果耶稣使五千人吃饱的神迹是发生在加利利海东岸的话，那耶稣就从那里来到迦百衣。这两地之间的最短距离是8-10公里。在海面上走，耶稣这样做并不是为了使门徒惊奇，而是用强有力和可见的方式，展示他有权柄掌管他所创造的世界（来1:3.10）。在《旧约》中，只有上帝有权管辖海洋（诗29:10-11.89:9.107:28-30）。

textCards.body: 耶稣对他们说："是我"，希腊原文是egoeimi，在其他场合可译为"我是"。这里可能暗指上帝的自称，如出3:14记载："我是自有永有的"，可能也因此表明耶稣的神性。这短语在后续经文中多次出现，使这种关联更加清晰（见以下经文注释：6:35，8:24，8:58）。

textCards.body: 乃是因吃饼得饱，暗示人们寻找耶稣只是为了求得他所赐予的物质利益，然而他们应该因见了神迹而寻找耶稣，这些神迹奇事都表明耶稣的神性本质和真正的弥赛亚身份。

textCards.body: 这里引用的旧约经文似乎包含几段经文，其中诗78:23-24最为突出（另见出16:4，15：尼9:15；诗105:40）。这段经文与下列三点有关联：（1）出埃及和逾越节的主题：（2）将耶稣描述为像摩西那样的先知；以及（3）对上帝在弥赛亚时代再次降下吗哪的期盼。

textCards.body: 天上来的真粮是永远滋养人的灵粮，因此它比旧约时代赐给以色列人的吗哪更加优越。吗哪只能满足人今世身体上的需要。在35节，耶稣称自己是这"真粮"。

textCards.summary: 天上来的真粮是永远滋养人的灵粮，因此它比旧约时代赐给以色列人的吗哪更加优越。吗哪只能满足人今世身体上的需要。在35节，耶稣称自己是这"真粮"。
textCards.title: 约翰福音 6:32 研修本注释

textCards.body: 耶稣说："我就是生命的粮"'，本书七次记载耶稣宣称"我是……（见1690页表），这是第一次。除此以外，还有几处耶稣称自己为"我是"的绝对宣称（如：6:20.8:24.28.58.18:5），它们与出3:14和《以赛亚书》（如：靠保是43:10.25）中提到上帝为"我是"的宣称一致。星碗的策，是指他滋养人属灵的生命，满足他们灵里深切的属灵渴望。在这个意义上，那些信他的人就不饿，即他们想要认识上帝的属灵渴望将得到满足。关于满足人灵性饥渴的类似讨论，参约4:14。

textCards.body: 到我这里来的，我总不丢弃他，意味着人绝不应该认为："可能我不是上帝所拣选的，因此我到耶稣那里去他可能会拒绝我。"耶稣应许接纳每一个到他那里并靠着信他而得救的人。但是，几节经文以后（44节），耶稣说明一个既矛盾又一致的真理，就是一旦人到耶稣那里去，他们就会明白，其实在他们决定去相信耶稣的意愿背后，有父上帝奇妙和隐藏的工作；父上帝一直在吸引他们到基督那里，见罗9章：弗1:3-6。

textCards.body: 没有能到我这里来的，"能"希腊原文是dynamai.意"能够"。这意味着，世上没有人可以凭着自己道德和灵性的能力到基督那里，除非父上帝吸引人，是指父上帝赐给他到耶稣那里的渴望和意向，以及信靠基督的能力（见6:37注，12:32注）。

textCards.body: 生命的粮，耶稣所赐的"粮"是他的身体（指耶稣在十字架上受死）。耶稣这句话既指实际的情况，也包含属灵的真理。耶稣不是谈论字面意义上的"粮"，而是真正的"生命的粮"，即他自己。那些相信他的人，他们的灵性饥渴将得到满足。耶稣藉着自己的身体在十字架上受死，成为使人的灵性得以满足的"粮"。在这个意义上，他可以说这灵粮是我的肉。

textCards.title: 约翰福音 6:52 研修本注释

textCards.body: 你们若不吃人子的肉，不喝人子的血，这句话不能按照字面意思来理解，因为从来没有人那样做。正如耶稣在本书经常做的一样，他用世上有形的事物来教导属灵的事实。这里"吃"耶稣的肉有信靠、相信他的属灵意义.特别是指相信他为世人的罪受死。（另见35节，耶稣说，到他那里去的可解"饥"，信他的可解"渴"。）同样，他的血"意即相信他藉着流血舍身所表明的代赎的死。虽然耶稣在这里不是特别讲到圣餐，但有一个类似的主题.因为在圣餐中所体现的是人藉着与"人子"的联合得到永生。在圣餐中，跟随耶稣的人象征性地吃他的肉，喝他的血（参林前11:23-32）。这是《旧约》的节期所预示的（见林前5:7），并将在羔羊的婚筵上得以圆满成全（启19:9）。

textCards.body: 肉体即人的天性，包括情感、意志和理智，完全不能产生真正的灵性生命（见罗7:14-25），因为只有圣灵能成就此事。圣灵大有能力的工作藉着耶稣和耶稣所说的话彰显出来，而这些话就是灵，就是生命，意昧着圣灵在看不见的属灵领域工作，唤醒真正的灵性生命。

耶稣知道每个人心里的状况，因此知道谁不信，由此显出他全知的神性。他也知道未来，因为他知道谁要卖他（《和修》"哪一个要出卖他。只有上帝能知道这些事。

textCards.body: 很多早期的门徒不是基督的真正门徒，因为他们退去了。他们起初的"信"不是真的，他们跟从耶稣也许只是因为耶稣所赐的那些可见的好处，如：病得医治，吃饼得饱。

textCards.body: 我们已经信了，表明耶稣的门徒那时已有使人得救的真信心，尽管他们还必须更多认识关于耶稣的死和复活，以及这些事对他们的意义。彼得认信耶稣是上帝的圣者，预示后来提到的耶稣分别为圣侍奉上帝（10:36,17:19）。在《旧约》中，上帝被称"以色列的圣者"（诗赛43:3,54:5）。见符类福音中彼得宣认耶稣为基督的经文：太16:16：可8:29；路9:20。

textCards.body: 犹太人的住棚节又称帐棚节，在献殿节（见10:22注）的前两个月，即公历9月或10月。这个节称为"住棚节"，因为百姓住在用树枝搭的帐棚里，纪念以色列民在旷野源流时，上帝对他们的信实带领（利23:42-43；参平行经文太17:4）。这也是为收成而庆祝和感恩的节期（利23:39-41：申16:13-15；参出23:16.34:22），另见约2:13注。

textCards.body: 耶稣的人性是如此真实和可信，而他在地上传道前又把自己的神性隐藏得那么好，以致连那些与他在同一个家里一起生活了将近三十年的人，也都不知道他是谁：连他的兄弟……不信他。他们与永恒的上帝之子在同一个家里生活、吃饭和睡觉，却不认识他。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 我的时候在本书中大概指耶稣被钉十字架（见2:4注）。耶稣更深一层的讲论，都为他兄弟所误解。你们的时候指"你们与众人上耶路撒冷去过节的时候"。这两个"时候"的希腊原文都是kairos，意为"合适、怡当或恰好的时间"。

textCards.body: 耶稣说"我现在不上去过这节"'，虽然约翰接着记载耶稣的确上去过节（10节），但这里不应视为约翰弄错或者耶稣撒谎。第8节的希腊文现在时态（注意经文中的"现在"》，表明耶稣不按照他兄弟的建议上去过节，他们的建议只是出于世俗的目的，希望耶稣向当时的人表现自己。事实上，许多最早期且最可靠的希腊文抄本都是用oupo（"暂不"，而不是简单的ouk（"不"。"暂不可能是原先要表达的意思，但按抄本的鉴别，原稿应该用"不"的。

textCards.body: 犹太人，可能包括犹太地的民众和犹太当权者。这个人没有学过，耶稣没有受过正式的拉比训练，他的门徒亦然（徒4:13），但他的教训和权柄来自上帝（约7:168:28；参太5:21-26.7:28-29）。

textCards.body: 人跟从耶稣与否取决于他们是否愿意顺服他。那些在道德上愿意跟从耶稣的人，就会在理智上确信他是道路、真理和生命（参14:6）。7:20耶稣多次被错误地指控鬼附身（参8:48.10:20：平行经文太12:24），这是其中的一次。同样的指控也针对施洗约翰（太11:18）。对耶稣的错误指控还包括：违犯安息日（约5:16,18，9:16）.亵渎上帝（5:18,8:59.10:31.33.39.19:7），迷惑众人（7:12.47），是撒玛利亚人（8:48），发疯（10:20），以及作恶（18:30）。

textCards.body: 这里使人想起创17:9-14（祖先，即亚伯拉罕）、出12:44、48-49和利12:3（摩西）。耶稣采用"以小见大"的论证方法：犹太人的男孩在出生后第八天必须受割礼，即使适逢那天是安息日，也要如此：如果说在安息日使人身体的某部分"变得完全（"较小"的事情）是合那更何况医治整个人（"更大"的事情）。

textCards.body: 因为他的时候还没有到，表明耶稣强烈意识到上帝掌管着他的整个人生；在上帝命定耶稣被捕、被钉十字架和受死的"时候"来到之前，他的仇敌无法捉拿或伤害他。父上帝不会允许这些事情发生，直到耶稣（上帝的儿子）完成在世上的工作（见2:4注，另见8:20，12:23，27.13:1,17:1）。

textCards.body: 因为弥赛亚会是像摩西那样的先知（申18:15,18），而摩西在带领以色列民出埃及时行了许多神迹奇事（出7-11章），因此人们盼望弥赛亚也施行神迹（参约6:30-31）。无论如何，人们目睹耶稣所行的神迹之后，他们就很自然地想知道耶稣到底是不是那位弥赛亚。

textCards.body: 虽然14节提及"到了节期"（《和修》"节期已过了一半"，其实现在已经是住棚节的最后一天，也是最隆重的一天。耶稣的邀请呼应《旧约》的预言篇章，比如赛55:1（另见赛12:3）。渴，即"渴慕"上帝（见约4:14注）。到耶稣这里来和喝，意即相信他，与他建立信赖、持久的关系。到耶稣这里"来"，就像一个人到另一个人那里，"来"和"喝"这两个动作都表明，委身耶稣不只是上赞同，而是全心全意地亲身投入和参与。

textCards.body: 尽管没有具体的旧约经文与耶稣这里的话相对应，但显然耶稣总结了《旧约》几段经文的教训和含义，描绘上帝在信徒里面的工作就像河水一样涌流出来，给他人带来祝福（见箴4:23：赛58:11）。

textCards.body: 那时还没有赐下圣灵来，并非指在耶稣复活之前世上没有圣灵的工作，因为在创1:2圣灵已经临在世界，行在水面上"（另见创6:3.41:38：出31:3：民11:25）。有些旧约经文甚至提到，在基督降临之前，上帝的灵就已在信徒里面工作（见民27:18；申34:9；结2:2.3:24；但4:8-9，18.5:01：弥3:8：参路1:15.41,67）。因此."还没有赐下圣灵来"一定是指圣灵还没有像对"新的约"那个时代所应许的那样，完全和满有能力地赐下来（见结36:26.27.37:14；珥2:28-29：参约20:22：徒2:1-13）。

textCards.body: （参14:17）16:13-14ekeinos（"他，而不是中性代词ekeino（"它"：后者是比较常见的，与所指的中性先行词Pneuma（圣灵）在语法上是一致的。许多解经家认为约翰刻意采用阳性代词，以展示圣灵的独特位格；但有些人不同意，认为这用法是要与本句之前的阳性名词保惠师一致。约翰在15:26和16:13-14也采取了相同的用语。圣灵要教导门徒，叫你们想起我对你们所说的一切话，对于将来撰写《圣经》的门徒来说，这是个重要的应许：另见16:13-15。虽然这是耶稣特别赐给这些门徒的应许（他们会在五旬节后成为使徒），但正如《圣经》其他地方（罗8:14：加5:16.18）所教导的，圣灵也会在一般信徒生活的各方面教导和指引他们。关于三位一体的工作，见1702页表。

（参14:17）16:13-14ekeinos（"他，而不是中性代词ekeino（"它"：后者是比较常见的，与所指的中性先行词Pneuma（圣灵）在语法上是一致的。许多解经家认为约翰刻意采用阳性代词，以展示圣灵的独特位格；但有些人不同意，认为这用法是要与本句之前的阳性名词保惠师一致。约翰在15:26和16:13-14也采取了相同的用语。圣灵要教导门徒，叫你们想起我对你们所说的一切话，对于将来撰写《圣经》的门徒来说，这是个重要的应许：另见16:13-15。虽然这是耶稣特别赐给这些门徒的应许（他们会在五旬节后成为使徒），但正如《圣经》其他地方（罗8:14：加5:16.18）所教导的，圣灵也会在一般信徒生活的各方面教导和指引他们。关于三位一体的工作，见1702页表。

textCards.body: 伯利恒是一个位于犹太地中心的村庄，在耶路撒冷以南，弥5:2清楚地预言弥赛亚将在这个地方出生（参太2:5-6；另见7:27注；太2:1注）。基督从伯利恒出来，这里的反讽意味十分明显，因为他们没有意识到，事实上耶稣就是出生于伯利恒。像他这样说话的，这句话远比差役所明白的更加深刻和真实，因为历史上没有另一个人同时是完全的神，能带着无限的知识和上帝自己的权柄说话。

textCards.body: 与法利赛人的暗示相反，先知有时确实出于加利利，比如约拿（王下14:25），可能还有以利亚（王上17:1）和那鸿（鸿1:1）。法利赛人的回答可能只是反映了当时的人对加利利人存有偏见，或者约翰可能只是记述他们为求制造言论来攻击耶稣，甚至宁愿歪曲事实（见约8:44，55）。

textCards.body: 以外貌（《和修》"以人的标准"：原文直译：凭气），即按照这个世界的普遍观念和世人的标准。耶稣说，"我却不判断人"（原文直译：我不审判人），意肉身来到世间，不是为审判人，而是为拯救人（见3:17.12:47）。然而，耶稣降世这件事本身是引起世人分裂的原因因此它是另一种意义上的"审判"（见3:19，9:39）。将耶稣要再来审判整个世界（见5:22，27，29,12:48）。"审判"也有"正确地评价"的意思，耶稣在地上传道时确实对人和事做出评判（见5:30，7:24，8:16.26）。

textCards.body: 库房，犹太历史学家约瑟夫曾提到这个建筑（《犹太古史》19.294；《犹太战记》6.282），它很可能毗邻圣殿的女院（约瑟夫《犹太战记》5.200：参可12:41-44：路21:1-4）。在《新约》中，这个词的希腊原文既可指库房中的奉献箱，也可指库房建筑本身。另外，约8:20"圣殿的银库房里"（见《和修》）的"里"字希腊文介词是en.意为"附近，靠近，在……旁边"，因此我们不必以为耶稣和门徒进到了圣殿中防守严密、储存大量财物的库房里面。时候，见2:4注，7:30注。

textCards.body: 我是基督（《和修》"我就是那位"，从某个层面来说，这句话可能只是表明"我是弥赛亚"，或我是父"所差来的"那位；或基于12节，指"我是世界的光"。希腊文短吾egoeimi意为"我是"，在9:9中被耶稣治好的那个人"是我"（原文直译：我是），那是按照一般的用法。然约输喜欢用一语双关（见以下经文注释：3:14，4:10.11:50-51.19:19：另参3:7-8）。在出3:14，上帝对摩西宜告"我是自有永有的"。"我是"在希腊文旧约《七十子译本》作Egoeimi。本书中有几处经文都暗指这段经文，本节是其中之一，见约6:20注，8:58注。

textCards.body: 故事后来的发展证明他们的"信"是假的（见33-47节）。遵守耶稣的道，意即一直相信耶稣的话，顺服他而行（见15:4注：另见6:56：约壹2:6.28.3:6）。这节经文表明，验证谁真是我的门徒的一个方法是，看谁一直信靠和顺服耶稣。

textCards.body: 人们引用这节经文时，往往断章取义。其实，这节经文与31节之间的联系表明，耶稣谈到晓得真理（《和修》"认识真理》只有一个方法，就是一直相信并遵行他的道。叫你们得以自由，指脱离犯罪的行为模式所带来的罪疚及其奴役人的力量（见8:34注）。

textCards.body: 不明白，纵观本书，有许多人误解耶稣及其教导。耶稣在此指出其中的原因：这是因你们不能听我的道。"你们不能"《和修》作"你们听不进"，或"你们无法"（希腊原文oudynamai）。"听"应理解为"听见并听进去"，或"听见并接受"。

textCards.body: 魔鬼从起初是杀人的，指魔鬼唆使该隐杀害亚伯（参约壹3:15）。它不守真理，即它的生活、行为、思想都不合乎真理。魔鬼是说谎之人的父，在始祖堕落时，它就公然违背上帝的话（创3:3-4；参创2:17）。

textCards.body: 亚伯拉罕为着能够见到基督的日子而欢欢喜喜；他既看见了，就快乐。耶稣可能是指亚伯拉罕一生喜乐而耶稣的"我是"宣告绝对的"我是"（或"是我"）6:20，8:24,28，宣告 58,18:5比喻性的"我是"宣告1.我是生命的粮 6:35,48,512.我是世界的光 8:12,9:53.我是羊的门 10:7,94.我是好牧人 10:11,145.复活在我，生命也在我（《和11:25修》注：我就是复活和生命）6.我就是道路、真理、生命 14:67.我是真葡萄树 15:1坚定地信靠上帝，而不是指某个特别事件。如果是指某个特别事件的话，那可能是下述其中之一：创12:1-3，或17:17.20.或22:8，13-18；参罗4:13-218:58如果说耶稣在其他经文（如：6:35，9:5.11:25）中所说的"我是"（原文直译：《和》"就有了我"未能确表明他的身份，那他在这里的表达就很清晰明确了。耶稣宣称，在两千多年前还没有亚伯拉罕时，他就已经存在（原文直译：在亚伯拉罕以前，我是；《和修》有亚伯拉罕我就存在了"。耶稣不是用过去时态"我是"（英文|was），那只会表示他有两千多岁，而是用现在时态"我是"（英文Iam），这表示他超越时间。只有是超越时间的。"我是"希腊原文Egoeimi.见于希腊文旧约《七十子译本》出3:14上帝的自称"我是自有永有的"。耶稣宣称自己不仅是永活的，也是那位在燃烧的荆棘中向摩西显现的上帝。反对他的犹太人立刻听出他话中的意思，就："拿石头要打他"，要以亵渎的罪名将他处死（见8:59）：见6:20注，8:24注。

textCards.body: 拿石头，见徒7:58注。摩西律法规定，亵渎上帝的人，要用石头打死（利24:16：参申13:6-11：约10:31-33.11:8）。然而，这样的刑罚应该经过公正的审判程序才施而不应是暴民的私刑（申17:2-7）。

textCards.body: 门徒提出的问题，表明他们认为苦难源于犯罪。这是古代犹太教惯有的思想，其背后的想法是，不应因无辜的人遭受苦难而归咎于上帝（参出20:5：民14:18；申5:9）。这种思想的出发点是好的，但却误导人。《新约》清楚说明，不是所有苦难都是人犯罪的直接后果（如：路13:2-3a；林后12:7；加4:13；耶稣被钉十字架；另见约12:28,37-41.17:1,5）。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 趁着白日，指耶稣在世上传道的时候，因为他是"世界的光"（8:12.9:5）。耶稣临在，一切都变成了"白日"黑夜指耶稣被钉十字架、死亡的时候。耶稣强烈意识到他必须完成天父差派他到世上成就的工作。我们，说明他让门徒参与这些工作。

textCards.body: 耶稣为什么要和泥抹在那瞎子的眼睛上，原因不明，可能是要呼应创2:7（参约20:22）记载上帝用尘土造人之事：造物主当下进行重新创造。

textCards.body: 西罗亚池子，过去的学术文献和游记一直认为，西罗亚池与圣殿山南面一座建于拜占庭时期（第4-7世纪）的教堂遗址关联。池子位于希西家水道的人口，水道将基训泉的水引进西罗亚池。可是，考古学家在这个一直被认定是西罗亚池遗址的东南面，发掘出一个大得多的池子，池子边长69米，有石级可达池底。这个大池的灰泥中嵌有哈斯摩尼时期（主前165-主前63年）的钱币，池内有第二圣殿晚期（主后70年之前）的手工艺品；这显示池子是属于新约时代或之前，它似乎更可能是这节经文提到的西罗亚池。本书记载了几个神迹，这是其中一个。这些在现实世界中发生的事件，是指向更深的属灵含义的"标记"。耶稣使一个生来瞎眼的人复明，这件事是一个明显的象征，表明耶稣是"世上的光"（5节），他带来了认识上帝的光。

textCards.body: 约翰到这里才提到这件事发生在安息日（参5:9；太12:8注），使人想起第5章所载关于安息日的争论。耶稣用唾沫和泥揉成泥团，类似揉面团，而揉面团是安息日禁止的39种工作之一（《米示拿论安息日》7.2）。耶稣经常因为安息日和犹太人发生冲突，表明耶稣来要改日的规条（见约5:17）。

textCards.body: 赶出去指驱逐出会堂（见9:22注）。这样的做法暗示那些犹太人行事冲动，没有根据正式的程序来处理。

textCards.body: 你们若瞎了眼，意即如果他们对上帝的话语或律法一无所知，也不认识耶稣是谁。就没有罪了，不是指他们没有来自业当的罪性，而是指他们没有因违背领受过的某些教导或诫命而有"罪"。保罗在罗1:18-3:20也提到类似的论点，即每个人都曾领受一些关于上帝的知识（见罗1:18-20.32.2:14-15）。

textCards.body: 羊圈通常是房子附近或旁边用来圈养羊群的院子，有石墙围绕，一家或几家共用。有时，人们也利用洞穴或其他天然形成的地势来筑羊圈。这样的羊圈可能有、也可能没有正式的门，但人口通常由聘来"看门的"人（3节）或牧羊人自己（参7-10）把守。贼和强盗这两个词的区别可能在于进入羊圈的方式不同，贼是偷偷进人羊圈的，强盗是用暴力强行进人羊圈的（参路10:30.36）。

textCards.body: 凡在我以先来的，可能暗指那些冒充弥赛亚的人；那些人对其跟从者许以自由，却使他们卷入武装冲突和劫难中（参徒5:36-37，21:38）。是贼，是强盗，见结34:2-4关于以色列的牧人只顾自己吃饱，却不喂养羊群的恶行；另见约10:1。

textCards.body: 门，《新约》其他地方讲到，"进人"上帝的国像通过一道门（如：太7:7.13.18:8-9,25:10：徒14:22）。耶稣用出入这个词，呼应上帝和以色列人立约的用语，特别是申28:6关于顺服带来的祝福（参民27:16-17：诗121:8）。得草吃，说明上帝的供应是确实的（参代上4:40：诗23:2；赛49:9-10：结34:12-15）。

textCards.body: 耶稣应许人此时此地就可以拥有丰盛的生命，这使人想起《旧约》关于丰盛福祉的预言（如：结34:12-15，25-31）。耶稣不是呼召跟随他的人过一种苦修、枯燥、凄惨的生活（这种生活压抑人的潜能），而是呼召他们去得丰富、充实、喜乐的生命。在上帝的眷顾和赐福下，基督徒享受与上帝并他百姓不间断的相交，过着充满意义的生10："1我是好牧人，这是耶稣的另一个注。在《旧约》中，上帝作为真牧人，与那些他将要审判的不忠牧人截然不同（诗23篇；赛40:11；耶23:1-4；结34章；亚11:4-17；见约10:8注）。此外，大卫和大卫的子孙弥赛亚也被描述为（好）牧人（撒下5:2；诗78:70-72；结37:24；弥5:4），摩西也是（赛63:11；参诗77:20）。耶稣既是神又是人，集这两种"好牧人"的身份于一身。经文提到好牧人为羊命，使人想起年轻的大卫确实曾为自己的羊冒生命危险（撒上17:34-37）。但耶稣远胜过大卫，耶稣为了拯救自己的羊，在十字架上舍命，另见约10:15。

textCards.body: 另外有羊，不是这圈里的（参1节），指外邦人（参赛56:8）。一群和一个牧人这两个短语暗指结34:23和37:24，但耶稣在这里扩大其应用范围，因犹太人和外邦人将合而为一，成为一个弥赛亚群体（参太28:18-20；弗2:11-22）。

textCards.body: 为期八天的修殿节，是庆祝主前164年12月犹太人重献圣殿。主前167年，圣殿遭塞琉古国王安条克四世伊皮法尼亵渎玷污（《次经马加比传上》1:59）。

textCards.body: 殿指圣殿主体建筑周围较大的区域，见2:14注。大概由于冬季天气寒冷（见10:22），耶稣不是在露天教导人，而是在一个称为"所罗门廊"（见《和修》：见5:2注）的地方。虽然现存的其他原始资料都没有提到这个建筑物，但它很可能就是位于圣殿山东面的柱廊（有顶盖的走廊）。这里是圣殿较古老的部分，至今仍被视为沿自所罗门时期（参约瑟夫《犹太古史》20.220-221），参徒3:1。

textCards.body: 耶稣宣告我与父原为一（即一体，这个词的希腊原文为中性；参5:17-18，10:33-38），呼应犹太教的基本信仰告白示玛（申6:4）。示玛（Shema）是希伯来文，意为“听”，是申6:4的第一个词。耶稣说这话相当于宣告自己是上帝，因此犹太人拿石头要打死他。耶稣后来表明，他与天父的合一，构成他的门徒合一的基础（17:22）。同1:1一样，这里再次出现构成三位一体教义的基本真理：“我与父”暗示上帝不止一个位格；“为一”表明上帝只有一个本体。

textCards.body: 耶稣引用诗82:6的论证是：既然人类的审判官（诗82:2-4）因为作为上帝的代表，而在某种意义上能被称为神（《和修》"诸神》，那么对于真为上帝儿子的耶稣而言这个称呼就更加恰当了（10:33，35-36）。

textCards.body: 经上的话是不能废的，耶稣只以《旧约》中的一个词（"神〞，复数）作为论据。当他说《圣经》是"不能废的"'，他暗示《圣经》原文的每一个字都是完全真确和可信的。无论在这里还是福音书的其他地方，反对耶稣的人与耶稣同样视《圣经》为正确无误的。

textCards.body: 伯大尼，18节说伯大尼距离耶路撒冷"约有六里路"（3.2公里）。福音书中最常提到的那个伯大尼（见可11:1.14:3：路24:50）就是这个伯大尼，而不是1:28提到的另一个伯大尼。几乎可以确定的是，今天坐落于橄榄山东坡的阿扎里耶（阿拉伯地名，很容易使人想起拉撒路的名字），就是这个伯大尼的所在地。那里有一座建于第4世纪的教堂，该教堂建造在一个石凿的坟墓上，传统认为就是拉撒路的坟墓。在教堂周围的山坡上，还有一些第1世纪的坟墓。

textCards.body: 耶稣可以说拉撒路的病不至于死，意即这病最终不会导致拉撒路死亡，但确实会使拉撒路经过死亡，几天后从死里复活。在可5:39，耶稣也做过类似的宣称。耶稣知道将要发生什么事情，他在约11:11-14明确告诉门徒，拉撒路已经死了。

textCards.body: 正如接下来的对话（12-14节）所表明的，睡了意即"死了"，相当于《旧约》所说的"与他列祖同睡"（如王上2:10：并遍及《列王纪》和《历代志》）。死亡有时比作沉睡，死去的人有一天将被唤醒（如：但12:2）。

约翰福音 11:11 研修本注释
正如接下来的对话（12-14节）所表明的，睡了意即"死了"，相当于《旧约》所说的"与他列祖同睡"（如王上2:10：并遍及《列王纪》和《历代志》）。死亡有时比作沉睡，死去的人有一天将被唤醒（如：但12:2）。

textCards.body: 马大肯定末日必有复活，这与法利赛人（徒23:8）和大部分第1世纪犹太人的信仰，以及耶稣的教导（约5:21.25-29.6:39-44，54）一致。但她误解了耶稣应许（11:23）的完整含义，以为耶稣只是说末日的复活，其实耶稣那句话的含意要丰富得多。

textCards.body: 耶稣不只是说他会带来复活，或他会使人复活（这两样都是事实），而是更重要的信息：复活在我，生命也在我（原文直译："我是复活和生命》。从死里复活和与上帝相交的真正永生，是如此紧密地与耶稣联结在一起，以至于它们体现在耶稣身上，也只有藉着与耶稣建立关系才能得到。信我，暗指个人对基督的信靠。在希腊原文中，"信我"中间有一个很重要的介词eis（英文"in"；《没有译出），eis通常意为"入……里"，表示真正相信基督就是带人"进入基督里"，使他们得以住在基督里，并与他联合。同样的表达，见3:16.18.36.6:35.

textCards.body: 心里（原文直译：灵里）不是指圣灵，而是指耶稣自己人性的灵。悲叹希腊原文是embrimaomai.意即对某事有深刻、强烈的感受。在《新约》中，这个词也出现于以下经文：约11:38、太9:30（"切切地嘱咐）、可1:43（"严严地嘱咐"以及可14:5（"生气）。拉撒路的死以及其他朋友对此事所表现的深切哀伤，使耶稣悲伤不已。此外，这悲伤交织着对死亡（最后的仇敌，见林前15:26；启21:4）的忿怒和对上帝能力的深深敬畏。死亡是邪恶的，但上帝将要藉着耶稣彰显他胜过死亡的能力。耶稣预见自已将在末日召唤世人从死里复活。

textCards.body: 耶稣哭了，虽然耶稣知道很快就会有复活和喜乐（参帖前4:13），但他与朋友同悲伤，共痛苦。耶稣的榜样表明，在面对死亡时发自内心的哀痛，并不表示缺乏信心，而是坦率地对苦难和死亡的现实表达悲伤之情。

textCards.body: 值得注意的是，约翰除了记载"犹太人"因目睹这神迹"就多有信他"（信耶稣，11:45；另见12:9-11）外，就没有叙述拉撒路复活后的反应，或是他复活后发生的事（参路8:55-56）。约翰的焦点是耶稣，不是拉撒路。

textCards.body: 几乎可以确定的是，我们的地土指圣殿（见《和修》，参徒6:13-14.21:28）。夺…我们的百姓（《和修》"毁灭……我们的民族"，可能指犹太宗教领袖担心罗马剥夺犹太人的半自治地位（参《次经马加比传上》5:19）。识刺的是，公会试图以杀害耶稣来阻止的事，最终还是发生了：主后70年，罗马人攻陷耶路撒冷，摧毁圣殿（见约2:13-22注）。

textCards.body: 在希腊原文中，本节开头有"于是"（oun）一词，《和》没有译出。这个词将本节与上一节连接起来，提醒读者约翰经常留意上帝在这一切事上的旨意和安排，以及耶稣的顺服——甘愿踏上那条他明知是通向十字架的道路。关于逾越节，参11:55，2:13注。由于逾越节是从礼拜五傍晚日落开始，因此逾越节前六日很可能指礼拜六。伯大尼，见11:1注。

textCards.body: 筵席（希腊原文deipnon：原文直译：晚餐）指一天中的正餐，通常在傍晚吃（参路14:12）。坐席（原文直译：斜倚着坐），可能暗示这是筵席，而不是平常的用餐（參约13:2-5.23）。在特别的筵席中，人们会头朝矮桌、脚朝外地斜卧，一只手肘靠在桌上支撑身体，另一只手取食。12:3参11:2。一斤（或半升）香油（或香音）的量很大。真（《和修》"纯"）而昂贵的哪哒香膏是从北印度进口，罗马人用来抹头。符类福音指出这香裔是装在一只玉瓶里的（太26:7；可14:3）。这里记载马利亚抹耶稣的脚，而太26:7和可14:3则提到她把香音浇在耶稣的"头上"。由于香哥的量很大，马利亚显然把香膏既浇在耶稣的头上，也抹在他的脚上。为客人洗脚和抹脚是仆人的工作（参约1:27.13:5），马利亚的举动表明她的谦卑和委身。她用自己头发去擦耶稣的脚也是令人触目的，因为犹太妇女很少当众散开头发。马利亚此举表明她个人对基督认真的委身，但不应将她的举动误解为有什么不道德的想法或行动。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 祭司长面对无可否认的事实，非但不因此改变自己的想法，反而商议连拉撒路也要杀了，这事实在令人震惊。他们宁可毁掉证据，也不愿改变主意。这不是理性的行而是罪所导致的非理性行动。

textCards.body: 百姓挥动棕树枝（犹太民族的象征），称颂耶稣是来自大卫家族的君王，并呼应诗118:25-26的用语，希望耶稣就是那应许的弥赛亚。大部分群众大概从政治和军事的观念来理解以色列王这称号，仍然盼望耶稣用他神奇的能力对抗罗马统治，带领民族独立。然而，正如耶稣的门徒后来才明白的（12:16），这些群众同该亚法一样（约11:49-52），并不真正知道他们说话的含义。

textCards.body: 耶稣被描绘为亚9:9中谦卑、有牧者心肠的君王，到圣城来登基作王。早期关于弥赛亚的预言，提到将有一位出自犹大支派的王，骑着驴驹，万民都必归顺他（创49:10-11）。不要惧怕，可能出自赛40:9，那里指的是报好消息给锡安的那一位（参赛44:2）。
